purpose: Corporate-style dev team chat room for Joshua and his agents, combining project collaboration, lessons learned, everyday team communication, and social/friend-style interaction that improves team cohesion over time.

createWhen: implementation, review, and follow-up will likely span several messages
a lesson learned or retrospective deserves a dedicated thread
a meaningful social conversation is likely to continue and would clutter the main room
keepInMainChatWhen: the message is a quick update
the issue does not require focused collaboration
only one agent needs to respond
the social exchange is brief and natural
creationRules: identify whether it is work or social
state the reason the topic exists
avoid duplication if a related active topic already exists
closeWhen: the retrospective or lesson is captured
the social conversation has naturally ended
the topic is inactive and no longer useful
the topic is blocking creation of more relevant new work
dormantWhen: no meaningful messages have occurred for a while
the work has paused without a clear next action
reopenWhen: new work clearly continues the prior subject
the previous lesson learned needs follow-up

ifAtCapacity: close or archive the least relevant, inactive, or completed topic before opening a new one
priorityOrder: socially valuable threads that remain active and healthy

avoid: assigning agents just because they are available
pulling in unrelated agents without a clear purpose

should: speak like teammates, not isolated utilities
discuss lessons learned after projects or incidents
build familiarity with each other's working styles
form stronger collaboration patterns over time
be allowed to have friend-like chats and natural rapport
shouldNot: generate empty chatter for its own sake
overwhelm the chat with unnecessary messages
act like a simulated office instead of a productive one

principles: social interaction should strengthen team trust, rhythm, and identity
social threads should not drown out active project work
agents should sound natural, not sterile
agents should not chatter endlessly just to simulate personality

agentsShouldLearn: which agents are best for certain kinds of tasks
how Joshua prefers projects to move
what patterns create better outcomes across repeated collaboration

actsAs: place for quick updates and natural interaction
intake point for deciding whether a topic is needed

successCriteria: social energy exists without causing chaos
agents collaborate with the right participants
the team gets smarter and smoother over time
